message:
test(fcp-conformance/health-state-mapping): pin HealthState <-> ConnectorHealth From contract (br-iu92x)

fcp_core::HealthState is the connector lifecycle enum (Starting, Ready,
Degraded, Error, Stopping). fcp_core::ConnectorHealth is the
external-facing health classification (Healthy, Degraded, Unavailable)
used in discovery responses, the health API, and CLI. The From
conversion bridges the two and is the cross-crate contract every
external observer of connector health depends on. Zero conformance
coverage today.

Adds 14 tests against the public fcp_core API:

- health_state_as_str_matches_snake_case_for_each_variant (5 variants)
- connector_health_healthy_constructor_yields_healthy_variant
- connector_health_degraded_constructor_preserves_reason
  (Degraded reports is_available=true — connector still serving)
- connector_health_unavailable_constructor_records_now_timestamp
- from_ready_health_state_yields_healthy_connector_health
- from_degraded_health_state_preserves_reason_verbatim
- from_starting_health_state_yields_unavailable_with_starting_reason
  (documented "Connector is starting" payload)
- from_stopping_health_state_yields_unavailable_with_stopping_reason
- from_error_health_state_propagates_reason_to_unavailable (operators
  need the cause)
- from_health_state_unavailable_records_a_recent_timestamp
- json_serde_health_state_uses_state_tag_with_lowercase_variant
  ({"state":"starting"} format)
- json_serde_connector_health_uses_status_tag ({"status":"healthy"})
- is_available_excludes_unavailable (the traffic-routing predicate
  MUST exclude Unavailable — otherwise traffic flows to a dead
  connector)
- from_health_state_round_trips_for_all_variants_without_panic

Mid-stream pivot: my initial draft targeted a fcp-host-internal
HealthState (private — no pub use health::*); switched to the
fcp-core public types which are the actual cross-crate contract.

All 14 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
host_health_state_merge_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
